text: fat is 22.3g. omega3 is where the section starts to wobble. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
carbs is 44.2g. fibre is where the section starts to wobble. for a seed, this is a very strong sign.
protein is 16.7g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
vitamin e at 18% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
magnesium at 92% DV, iron at 55% DV, calcium at 30% DV stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

on the plus side. tiny food with serious nutrient density because seeds often overperform for their size. easy to sprinkle into meals because that practical versatility matters. usually brings decent fat support because that is one of the category strengths.

the drawbacks next. calorie density still stacks up fast because tiny foods can still overshoot fast. real serving sizes are often small because some strong paper numbers are hard to cash out in practice. texture or prep can be a barrier because not everyone uses seeds consistently.
Nigella Seeds is B tier. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
